answer:
The correct answer is E. Statement (ii) is true because variance decompositions show the proportion of forecast error variance due to each variable. Statement (iv) is also true, as in practice a variable’s own shocks typically explain most of its forecast error variance, especially at short horizons. Statements (i) and (iii) are false: (i) describes impulse responses, not variance decompositions, and (iii) is incorrect because ordering matters for both impulse responses and variance decompositions in orthogonalized settings.

\boxed{E}
